title: Revised 2026 EU ETS auction calendars published
content: Today, the European Energy Exchange (EEX) published the revised 2026 EU ETS auction calendars.
snippet: Today, the European Energy Exchange (EEX) published the revised 2026 EU ETS auction calendars.

title: Commission seeks public opinion on how EU Nature Directives are working
content: A public consultation on the Birds and Habitats Directives is underway. Contributions will feed into a stress test of both.
snippet: A public consultation on the Birds and Habitats Directives is underway. Contributions will feed into a stress test of both.

title: Commission launches the European Housing Alliance
content: European Commissioner for Energy and Housing Dan Jørgensen is inviting Member States, regions, cities and stakeholders across the Union to join Europe’s first-ever Housing Alliance.
snippet: European Commissioner for Energy and Housing Dan Jørgensen is inviting Member States, regions, cities and stakeholders across the Union to join Europe’s first-ever Housing Alliance.

title: New EMN inform examines digital inclusion and accessibility in legal migration procedures
content: Legal migration procedures are becoming more digital. Learn more about accessibility, inclusion, challenges and support for applicants in the new EMN inform.
snippet: Legal migration procedures are becoming more digital. Learn more about accessibility, inclusion, challenges and support for applicants in the new EMN inform.

title: Lessons from Medieval Europe could guide modern approaches to balancing biodiversity and agriculture
content: Research from Germany suggests that plant biodiversity boomed in Europe’s Medieval centre, despite a concurrent rise in farming and trade – and that current land management policy could learn from the approaches of these long-passed times.

title: EU, Ukraine, Canada and partners join efforts to return forcibly transferred and unlawfully deported Ukrainian children
content: Today, representatives of the European Union together with Ukraine and Canada hosted a ‘High-level Meeting of the International Coalition for the Return of Ukrainian Children'. This initiative was announced by European Commission President, Ursula von der Leyen, in her 2025 State of the Union addres

title: EU coordinates international support for the recovery of Syria's economy and reconstruction process
content: The European Commission, together with the Syrian Transitional Authorities, hosted today the Syria Partnership Coordination Forum in Brussels. This meeting further delivered on the main commitments of the Forum

title: Commission unconditionally approves Suzano's acquisition of Kimberly-Clark IFP
content: The European Commission has unconditionally approved, under the EU Merger Regulation, the proposed acquisition by Suzano S.A. ('Suzano') of Kimberly Clark IFP NewCo B.V. ('Kimberly-Clark IFP').
snippet: The European Commission has unconditionally approved, under the EU Merger Regulation, the proposed acquisition by Suzano S.A. ('Suzano') of Kimberly Clark IFP NewCo B.V. ('Kimberly-Clark IFP').

title: Protecting sea lanes through partnership: exercise Bell Buoy concludes in Auckland
content: Exercise Bell Buoy concluded in early May 2026 after two weeks of intense operational activity in Auckland, New Zealand, bringing together international military and maritime partners to strengthen cooperation in an increasingly complex global security environment.

title: New law strengthening water protection in EU enters into force
content: The new regulations will make Europe more water-resilient, while boosting its zero-pollution and water-resilience ambitions.
snippet: The new regulations will make Europe more water-resilient, while boosting its zero-pollution and water-resilience ambitions.

title: Commission reports on progress in implementing Pact on Migration and Asylum
content: On 8 May 2026, the Commission published a report on the state of play on the Pact on Migration and Asylum's implementation, showing that Member States have significantly advanced in implementing the Pact. However, continuous continuous efforts are needed for the Pact's full entry into application.

title: Commission publishes guidance to support EU transport sector affected by the Middle East crisis
content: The Middle East crisis continues to affect Europe’s transport and tourism sector, notably through disruptions to fuel supply and the closure of certain air and shipping routes.
snippet: The Middle East crisis continues to affect Europe’s transport and tourism sector, notably through disruptions to fuel supply and the closure of certain air and shipping routes.

title: Side by Side: Testimonials of EU and non-EU citizens working together
content: EU businesses face labour shortages across many sectors. Workers from outside the EU are helping to fill this gap. Side by Side video series show how non-EU workers contribute their skills to EU businesses, helping address labour shortages through successful training and integration.
snippet: EU businesses face labour shortages across many sectors. Workers from outside the EU are helping to fill this gap. Side by Side video series show how non-EU workers contribute their skills to EU busin

title: Partnerships at the centre of employment service delivery: insights from the PES Network Annual Stakeholder Conference
content: The Public Employment Services (PES) Network has just released a Synthesis Paper capturing the outcomes of the Annual Stakeholder Conference, “Partnerships in shaping and delivering employment services”, held on 21 October 2025 in Brussels.
snippet: The Public Employment Services (PES) Network has just released a Synthesis Paper capturing the outcomes of the Annual Stakeholder Conference, “Partnerships in shaping and delivering employment service

title: Golf industry professional builds cross-border career with EURES support
content: Spaniard Juan Jesús Toscano works in Portugal’s leading golf resort, after advice from the EURES cross-border partnership Andalucia-Algarve (EURES-T AA).
snippet: Spaniard Juan Jesús Toscano works in Portugal’s leading golf resort, after advice from the EURES cross-border partnership Andalucia-Algarve (EURES-T AA).

title: Commission launches call for applications for revised Expert Group on drones and innovative air mobility
content: The Commission is set to review the Drone Strategy 2.0 this year, to assess progress since its adoption in 2022 and identify possible adjustments or additional measures for the development of the EU’s drone market.

title: Ireland sees changes to migration policies and improved digital inclusion
content: The Irish government published a new policy document on family reunification, also announcing changes to the right to work for children of employment permit holders. It has also launched its International Protection Portal and waived registration fees for migrant victims of domestic abuse.

title: Sweden sees changes to integration policy and research on migrant youth
content: The Swedish government announces new initiatives to support the integration of migrant children and proposes a new law on the settlement of beneficiaries of international protection. It also proposed limiting access to welfare for newly arrived migrants by introducing new eligibility criteria.

title: Migration statistics and integration funding announced in Hungary
content: The EEA & Norway Grants Civil Society Fund Hungary launched a call to support initiatives cooperating with Ukrainian partners, providing support to build resilience and counter hostile narratives. The Hungarian government also published migration statistics for 2025 and an updated guide in English.
snippet: The EEA & Norway Grants Civil Society Fund Hungary launched a call to support initiatives cooperating with Ukrainian partners, providing support to build resilience and counter hostile narratives.

title: The hydrocopter: A new era in ice safety
content: In Estonia, what began as a fisher’s idea for survival has evolved into a professional vehicle designed for the world’s most unforgiving environments. The hydrocopter is a type of boat that utilises aerodynamic propulsion, much like a helicopter, rather than a traditional underwater motor

title: The young skipper from Culatra defending his island’s fishing identity
content: Fishing is woven into the identity of the small island of Culatra, but with fewer young fishers entering the profession, it is in danger of being lost. Coming from a long line of fishers, Hugo Padinha was determined not to let that happen.
snippet: Fishing is woven into the identity of the small island of Culatra, but with fewer young fishers entering the profession, it is in danger of being lost. Coming from a long line of fishers, Hugo Padinha

title: Choosing the right policy mix is essential to promote a circular economy
content: A new policy assessment to support the transition to circular economy has shown that a combination of approaches targeting consumption and production can reduce material extraction in Europe by 15% at an overall cost of about 1% of GDP.

title: Seeing is believing simply looking at green walls improves mood and creates a sense of cooling
content: New research shows that green walls and roofs are effective at both cooling the environment and offering physical and mental wellbeing benefits, reinforcing the value of green infrastructure in alleviating heat stress in cities.

title: Commission invites comments on draft revised State aid rules for air transport
content: The European Commission has today launched a public consultation inviting all interested parties to comment on its draft revised Guidelines on State aid to the air transport sector. These will replace the existing Guidelines, which were adopted in 2014.

title: Your skills are key to a brighter AI future
content: AI could be a force for good in our careers and lives. It could also lead us down a darker path. Read about three such futures that could become reality, all depending on the action Europe takes now.
snippet: AI could be a force for good in our careers and lives. It could also lead us down a darker path. Read about three such futures that could become reality, all depending on the action Europe takes now.

title: Commission approves €1.5 billion Spanish State aid scheme to support farmers affected by 2025-2026 floods in Andalusia and Extremadura
content: The European Commission has approved, under EU State aid rules, a €1.5 billion Spanish scheme to support farmers affected by a series of adverse meteorological events in the regions of Andalusia and Extremadura between 10 November 2025 and 9 February 2026.

title: European Youth Week highlights housing challenge for the next generation
content: Housing was amongst the topics discussed during the European Youth Week in late-April, where young people shared their experiences with the Commission and several other institutions.
snippet: Housing was amongst the topics discussed during the European Youth Week in late-April, where young people shared their experiences with the Commission and several other institutions.

title: Commission reinforces resilience of money market funds with new guidance
content: Today’s report provides, alongside the FAQs, key additional guidance and aims to support more consistent and well-calibrated supervision of MMFs across the EU, strengthening the resilience of the sector.

title: From Orbit to the Water Tap: 11th CASSINI Hackathon
content: From 24 to 26 April 2026, the 11th CASSINI Hackathon: Space for Water took place simultaneously across ten countries, bringing together 722 participants creating 145 teams to apply EU Space Programme technologies to one of the most pressing challenges of our time: water.

title: Summary report: Reality check on simplifying excise procedures
content: A reality check on simplifying excise procedures took place on 23 March.
